reasoning: Corporate bond yields largely follow a random walk over the short-to-medium term, putting the base-rate probability of an increase at ~50%. Historical seasonal data (indicated by the FRED tool, showing identical yields remaining elevated in late May/early June of the prior year) as well as a slight recent bump in late May (climbing from 4.85 on May 2 to 4.97 on May 21 before settling at 4.94) suggest weak near-term upward momentum, leading to a forecast slightly above 50% for June 1. Looking further out (to Q3/Q4 2025), expected interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve and ongoing softening of long-term bond yields point toward a neutral or slightly negative drift. In line with the instruction to increase uncertainty and bring distant dates closer to 0.5, the probabilities smoothly converge to 0.50.
